report_name: Q2 Ransomware Attacks Up 3%; VPNs Remain Top Initial Access Vector

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: The most prolific ransomware group in Q2 2026, claiming 301 victims (14% of the total). The group actively exploits vulnerabilities in VPNs and other edge devices for initial access.

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: The second most active ransomware group in Q2 2026, responsible for 238 attacks. This group is noted for exploiting vulnerabilities in VPN products.

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: The third most active ransomware group in Q2 2026, with 145 victims.

threat_actors.name: KryBit
threat_actors.description: A new Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that debuted in Q2 2026, claiming 56 victims.

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group actively exploiting vulnerabilities in VPN products from vendors like Fortinet, Citrix, SonicWall, and Check Point for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups like Qilin and Akira are actively exploiting vulnerabilities in internet-facing VPN products from vendors such as Fortinet, Citrix, SonicWall, and Check Point to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploit flaws in VPN appliances to bypass authentication mechanisms, including MFA, and gain direct access to internal corporate networks.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Used as a standard ransomware TTP for data exfiltration before encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the ransomware attack, where data is encrypted for extortion.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: Ransomware associated with the Qilin group, which was the most active threat actor in Q2 2026.

malware.name: Akira
malware.description: Ransomware associated with the Akira group, which actively exploits VPN vulnerabilities.

indicators.name: Potential VPN Exploitation Attempt via API Endpoint
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential VPN Exploitation Attempt via API Endpoint
id: 2b9e1f0a-3d7c-4b8e-9f6a-1c2d3e4f5a6b
status: experimental
description: Detects web requests to known vulnerable API endpoints on VPN appliances, which could indicate an exploitation attempt.
references:
  - https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/22/ransomware-attacks-rise-3-in-q2-as-supply-chain-compromises-escalate-ncc-group-warns/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '/api/v1/remediation'
      - '/remote/fgt_lang'
  condition: selection
level: high
indicators.description: Detects web requests to known vulnerable API endpoints on VPN appliances, based on hunting hints provided in the report.

indicators.name: Web Server Spawning Shell on Edge Device (Windows)
indicators.pattern:
title: Web Server Spawning Shell on Edge Device (Windows)
id: 8c4d5e6f-7a8b-4c9d-a1b2-c3d4e5f6a7b8
status: experimental
description: Detects a web server process on a Windows-based edge device (like a VPN appliance) spawning a command shell, which may indicate successful remote code execution after an exploit.
references:
  - https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/22/ransomware-attacks-rise-3-in-q2-as-supply-chain-compromises-escalate-ncc-group-warns/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
level: high
indicators.description: Detects a web server process on a Windows edge device spawning a shell, a strong indicator of RCE, based on hunting hints in the report.

indicators.name: Web Server Spawning Shell on Edge Device (Linux)
indicators.pattern:
title: Web Server Spawning Shell on Edge Device (Linux)
id: f9e8d7c6-b5a4-4b3c-8d2e-1f0a9b8c7d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects a web server process on a Linux-based edge device (like a VPN appliance) spawning a command shell, which may indicate successful remote code execution after an exploit.
references:
  - https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/22/ransomware-attacks-rise-3-in-q2-as-supply-chain-compromises-escalate-ncc-group-warns/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '/httpd'
      - '/apache2'
      - '/nginx'
    Image|endswith:
      - '/sh'
      - '/bash'
  condition: selection
level: high
indicators.description: Detects a web server process on a Linux edge device spawning a shell, a strong indicator of RCE, based on hunting hints in the report.

relationships.source_name: Potential VPN Exploitation Attempt via API Endpoint
Web Server Spawning Shell on Edge Device (Windows)
Web Server Spawning Shell on Edge Device (Linux)
